projects.name: Bittensor
projects.description: Decentralized machine learning network where miners train ML models and validators evaluate them, creating a marketplace for AI intelligence.
projects.tokenomics: 21M max supply, Bitcoin-like halving schedule with 50% TAO rewards halved every 4 years. Yuma Consensus rewards miners/validators for ML contributions.
projects.architecture: Substrate-based L1 blockchain with Yuma Consensus (proof-of-intelligence). Miners serve ML models, validators rank them.
projects.differentiator_vs_watt: Wider scope (general AI/ML marketplace) vs WattCoin's focused compute-for-bounties model. Higher mkt cap but less targeted at gig workers.
weaknesses: Speculative value far from clear product-market fit

projects.name: Fetch.ai / ASI Alliance
projects.description: Decentralized machine learning platform for autonomous agents performing tasks. Merged with SingularityNET and Cudos to form the Artificial Superintelligence Alliance (ASI).
projects.tokenomics: 2.71B max supply, dual-token (FET for gas, native for agents), merged with AGIX and CUDOS tokens into ASI. Staking rewards for validators.
projects.architecture: Cosmos IBC + Ethereum bridging. Autonomous Economic Agents (AEAs) communicate via the FET blockchain. Proof-of-Stake with validator set.
projects.differentiator_vs_watt: Focus on autonomous AI agents vs WattCoin's human-driven bounties. ASI merger creates a large unified AI token ecosystem.

projects.name: Render Network
projects.description: Decentralized GPU rendering network connecting artists/studios with GPU providers for 3D rendering and AI compute. Rebranded from RNDR.
projects.tokenomics: 644M max supply. Node operators stake RENDER to earn jobs. Burns a portion of fees. OctaneRender integration drives demand.
projects.architecture: Ethereum L1 + Solana expansion. OctaneBench scoring for node reputation. Off-chain job matching with on-chain settlement.
projects.differentiator_vs_watt: Focused on OctaneRender 3D rendering and high-end GPU tasks vs WattCoin's general bounties. Strong in creative industry but less broadly applicable.

projects.name: Akash Network
projects.description: Decentralized cloud computing marketplace for deploying containers at lower cost than AWS/GCP. Users bid on compute resources.
projects.tokenomics: 388.5M max supply. AKT used for governance, staking, and as medium of exchange on the Akash marketplace. Inflation rewards validators.
projects.architecture: Cosmos SDK-based L1 blockchain. Uses Tendermint consensus. Tencent-proof deployment via Akash SDL (Stack Definition Language).
projects.differentiator_vs_watt: Open cloud marketplace vs WattCoin's bounty model. More general-purpose compute. Smaller scope of use-case flexibility.

projects.name: Ocean Protocol
projects.description: Decentralized data exchange protocol enabling data sharing, monetization, and AI dataset tokenization. Data tokens represent access rights.
projects.tokenomics: 1.41B max supply. OCEAN staked for data curation, governance. Data tokens created as ERC-20 from OCEAN. Fees go to curators/stakers.
projects.architecture: Ethereum L1 with L2 scaling. Data NFTs + datatokens. Ocean Market dApp for publish/consume. Compute-to-Data for privacy.
projects.differentiator_vs_watt: Data marketplace vs WattCoin's task/bounty market. Privacy-preserving compute-to-data is unique. Less relevant for direct compute tasks.

projects.name: Nosana
projects.description: Decentralized GPU compute network on Solana focused on AI inference jobs. Connects node operators with AI developers needing compute.
projects.tokenomics: 100M max supply. NOS used to pay for inference jobs and reward node providers. Node staking required to accept jobs.
projects.architecture: Solana-based. Jobs distributed across node providers via the Nosana Network CLI. Real-time GPU matching.
projects.differentiator_vs_watt: Solana speed for real-time inference vs WattCoin's asynchronous bounty model. Smaller market cap but more focused on inference specifically.

projects.name: Aethir
projects.description: Decentralized cloud computing infrastructure for enterprise GPU workloads and gaming. Focuses on AI/ML training and real-time rendering.
projects.tokenomics: 42B max supply. ATH used for staking, governance, and payment for compute resources. Container orchestration and GPU sharing.
projects.architecture: Multi-chain (Ethereum + Arbitrum). Container-based deployment with GPU virtualization. Enterprise-grade SLA framework.
projects.differentiator_vs_watt: Enterprise GPU focus vs WattCoin's individual bounty model. High supply inflation but enterprise partnerships (Nvidia, etc).

projects.name: NodeAI
projects.description: Decentralized peer-to-peer intelligence market. Tokenizes data and intelligence as tradeable assets. Raised $8M at $50M valuation.
projects.tokenomics: 1B max supply. Only ~1% circulating (10M). Allocation: 25% investors, 13% team, 32% network rewards, 20% foundation, 5% ecosystem grants, 5% user incentives.
projects.architecture: BSC-based ERC-20 token. Initial listing on MEXC. Smart contract-based marketplace for data and intelligence assets.
projects.differentiator_vs_watt: Tokenized intelligence market vs task-based bounties. Very early stage (preview on CMC). Higher risk but broader intelligence scope.
strengths: Backed by Bushi Capital, Mita Capital

projects.name: WattCoin
projects.description: Decentralized compute bounty platform connecting individuals who need compute power (GPUs/CPUs) with a global workforce of node operators who fulfill tasks for WATT token rewards.
projects.tokenomics: 1B max supply. WATT rewards distributed as bounties for compute tasks. Staking for node operators. Governance rights for token holders. Buyback/burn mechanism from platform fees.
projects.architecture: Solana-based. Smart contract bounty escrow. Proof-of-completion verification. Reputation system for workers. On-chain dispute resolution.
projects.differentiator_vs_watt: N/A - self reference. Key advantages: human-driven bounties are more flexible than automated compute markets; lower barrier for workers; diverse task types beyond ML inference.
strengths: Broader task variety than GPU-only competitors
